sourceMovie.title: Outlander
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Outlander works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Predators
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Outlander excels in science-fiction, Predators (2010) doubles down on action to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Predator 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Predator 2 (1990) is the natural next step after Outlander: same science-fiction DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and action elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Stargate: Continuum
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Outlander who respond to its science-fiction will find Stargate: Continuum (2008) immediately familiar. Both films share a action sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Hulk vs. Wolverine
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Outlander fans keep landing on Hulk vs. Wolverine (2009) — the science-fiction is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Occupation: Rainfall
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Occupation: Rainfall draws from the same science-fiction well as Outlander, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2020 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: The 13th Warrior
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The 13th Warrior (1999) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Outlander later expanded. If Outlander resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Nimona
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Outlander — that funny edge, the animation — translates cleanly into Nimona (2023). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Critters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Critters (1986) clearly understood what makes science-fiction work — the same quality that keeps Outlander rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Splinter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Splinter (2008) is the kind of film Outlander fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The science-fiction overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Body Snatchers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Body Snatchers shares Outlander's gift for science-fiction — the 1993 film delivers horror without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Outlander?
faq.answer: Try Predators, Predator 2, Stargate: Continuum—each shares DNA with Outlander while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Outlander?
faq.answer: Outlander (2008) is best described as Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Outlander worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
